textPlain: This is really lovely work! Simple to use, surprisingly solid, and just a pleasure to poke around with. The fact it runs in the browser is a bit of magic on its own.One idea for later might be a few preset systems, such as Alpha Centauri or other known three-body systems. It would give people a quick way to drop into something real before they start making chaos of their own.Anyway, cracking project.
Nice work!Were you by any chance inspired to make this because of the three body series by Cixin Liu? Or were you moreso just inspired because the simulation/math/physics are interesting?

Thanks so much, really appreciate it! That’s a great idea. I’ve been focusing the presets on stable or interesting solutions that aren’t tied to real systems, but adding a few real examples like Alpha Centauri would fit in nicely. I’ll keep that on the list for future updates.

Thanks! The Three-Body series definitely helped spark the idea, and the URL is a little nod to the books. I also took some simulation classes back in college, so the math and physics side pulled me in too. It’s crossed my mind that it could be fun to add a kind of Trisolaran mode that tracks a small planet and how habitable its position is throughout the orbits.
I think the URL is telling
